subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate skills in dealing with environmental challenges
indicator_description: Examine water pollution as an environmental challenge
examplars:
1. Explain pollution. [cite: 1985]
2. Watch a documentary of polluted water bodies for discussion. [cite: 1985]
3. Discuss the causes of water pollution, including human, animal and industrial waste. [cite: 1991]
4. Examine the effects of water pollution, including death of fish and the contamination of water leading to diseases such as cholera, typhoid and dysentery. [cite: 1991]
5. Presentation on the need to prevent water pollution. [cite: 1991]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.1.1.2. Analyse the sources and ways of conserving energy in Ghana
indicator_description: Examine the means of conserving energy
examplars:
1. Explain energy conservation. [cite: 1997]
2. Discuss responsible use of energy to minimise economic and environmental costs. [cite: 1997]
3. Role play how different members of the community might conserve energy. [cite: 2001]
4. Debate the need for energy conservation. [cite: 2001]
5. Apply the knowledge and skills acquired to help conserve energy in learners' homes and school. [cite: 2001]
6. Project work: Use sun or wind to generate energy. [cite: 2007]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.1.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of the significance of weather and climate to the environment
indicator_description: Assess the significance of weather and climate to the environment
examplars:
1. Explain the concepts: i. Climate, ii. Weather. [cite: 2014]
2. Discuss the elements of climate, including temperature, humidity, atmospheric pressure and precipitation. [cite: 2014]
3. Discuss various ways of measuring the elements of weather and climate. [cite: 2014]
4. Sketch the map of Ghana and indicate the different climatic zones. [cite: 2019]
5. Discuss the world climatic zones. [cite: 2019]
6. Examine the factors that affect climate, including latitude, altitude and distance from the sea. [cite: 2019]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.1.3.2. Demonstrate understanding of natural disasters and their management
examplars:
1. Watch some documentaries on natural disasters. [cite: 2024]
2. Examine natural disasters and share the understanding of the term. [cite: 2024]
3. Examine the causes of flooding, including human action. [cite: 2030]
4. Investigate the effects of flooding on the environment. [cite: 2030]
5. Carry out a project to raise awareness in the community about the need for certain flood control measures. [cite: 2035]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.1.2.1. Demonstrate skills in sketching maps and interpreting landscapes from maps
indicator_description: Sketch maps and interpret landscapes from maps
examplars:
1. Sketch a map of your school compound. [cite: 2040]
2. Draw a diagram showing an eight-point compass. [cite: 2040]
3. Using a map with grid squares, locate some important landmarks. [cite: 2040]
4. Draw/sketch the map of the community indicating important landmarks. [cite: 2047]
5. Sketch a map of the school compound to scale. [cite: 2047]
6. Show directions to important places in the community. [cite: 2047]
7. Sketch maps showing the positions or directions to your home using compass directions. [cite: 2047]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.2.2.1. Show understanding of the family and family life issues
indicator_description: Examine the composition and functions of the nuclear and the extended families
examplars:
1. Explain the concept of family. [cite: 2052]
2. Differentiate nuclear from extended family systems. [cite: 2052]
3. Identify the roles of various members of the nuclear family. [cite: 2052]
4. Explain the roles of the extended family. [cite: 2052]
5. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the nuclear family systems. [cite: 2059]
6. Compare the Ghanaian family system and that of any country in the Western world. [cite: 2059]
7. In groups, discuss the importance of the family as an institution. [cite: 2059]
8. Discuss the role of the family in supporting gender equality. [cite: 2059]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.2.2.1. Show understanding of the family and family life issues
indicator_description: Discuss the issues of inheritance in Ghana
examplars:
1. In pairs, discuss the concept of inheritance. [cite: 2064]
2. Identify and discuss the systems of inheritance in Ghana. [cite: 2064]
3. Invite a resource person to talk about inheritance. [cite: 2064]
4. Discuss the positive and negative effects of the inheritance systems in Ghana. [cite: 2064]
5. Suggest ways of improving the indigenous inheritance system. [cite: 2064]
6. Role play one system of inheritance in Ghana. [cite: 2068]
7. Debate the Intestate Succession law versus the indigenous inheritance system in Ghana. [cite: 2068]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.2.4.1. Analyse the population structure in Ghana and its related issues
indicator_description: Compare the population structure of high-income and middle/low income countries
examplars:
1. Explain what population structure means. [cite: 2073]
2. Draw/sketch the population structure of your school. [cite: 2073]
3. Examine the population structure of Ghana as set out in a population pyramid. [cite: 2073]
4. Examine the population structure of any developed country. [cite: 2073]
5. Discuss the population structures of low, middle and high-income countries. [cite: 2073]
6. Suggest reasons for the population pyramids with different shapes. [cite: 2073]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.3.2.1. Demonstrate knowledge of the role of the individual in the community
indicator_description: Examine the role of the individuals in the community
examplars:
1. Explain the concept of community. [cite: 2080]
2. Watch a documentary about different communities. [cite: 2080]
3. Explain what community development involves. [cite: 2080]
4. Explain the factors to be considered in community decision-making process. [cite: 2087]
5. Invite a resource person to talk about community development. [cite: 2087]
6. Identify a specific community problem and help solve it. [cite: 2092]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.3.2.1. Demonstrate knowledge of the role of the individual in the community
indicator_description: Discuss the relevance of volunteerism to community development
examplars:
1. Explain the concept of volunteerism. [cite: 2092]
2. Examine ways by which one can volunteer responsibly. [cite: 2092]
3. Identify volunteering roles and their contribution to development. [cite: 2092]
4. Discuss the importance of volunteerism to socio-economic development. [cite: 2098]
5. Project work: form voluntary clubs. [cite: 2098]
6. Do a voluntary work in the community and write a report. [cite: 2098]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.4.1.1. Investigate the rights and responsibilities of a citizen
indicator_description: Examine the importance of human rights in the Ghanaian society
examplars:
1. Explain the concept "human rights". [cite: 2103]
2. Examine the United Nations declaration on human rights. [cite: 2103]
3. Identify human rights enshrined in the 1992 constitution. [cite: 2103]
4. Assess the importance of the protection of human rights. [cite: 2103]
5. Examine the rights and responsibilities of a citizen. [cite: 2110]
6. Role play rights and responsibilities. [cite: 2110]
7. Undertake a project on safeguarding citizens' rights. [cite: 2110]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.4.2.1. Analyse ways of preventing and managing conflict
indicator_description: Examine ways of preventing and managing conflict in the community
examplars:
1. Explain terms: Conflict, Conflict prevention, Conflict management. [cite: 2114]
2. Watch and discuss video on conflict effects. [cite: 2114]
3. Identify causes of conflict in your community. [cite: 2114]
4. Discuss effects of conflict in your community. [cite: 2121]
5. Examine attitudes and values needed to prevent conflict. [cite: 2121]
6. Assess the role of women in conflict management. [cite: 2121]
7. Suggest ways to manage conflict situations. [cite: 2125]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.5.2.1.Demonstrate understanding of employer and employee relations in social security and pension
indicator_description: Exhibit knowledge on employer and employee relation in social security and pension schemes
examplars:
1. Explain concepts: Employment, Employer, Employee. [cite: 2130]
2. Discuss obligations of an employer in social security. [cite: 2130]
3. Discuss obligations of an employee towards social security. [cite: 2130]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.5.3.1. Evaluate tourism as an important economic sector for national development
indicator_description: Assess the importance of tourism to socio-economic development of Ghana
examplars:
1. Identify tourist sites using videos/pictures. [cite: 2136]
2. Visit tourist sites and suggest improvements. [cite: 2136]
3. Discuss how new tourist sites can be developed. [cite: 2136]
4. Examine importance of tourism to socio-economic development. [cite: 2142]
5. Compare tourism development between Ghana, Kenya, and UK. [cite: 2142]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.6.2.1. Analyse the main developments in the Republics between 1960 and 1972
indicator_description: Explain how the First Republic came into being
examplars:
1. Research how the First Republic was established. [cite: 2147]
2. Discuss outcome of 27 April 1960 referendum. [cite: 2147]
3. Discuss outcome of 1960 presidential elections. [cite: 2147]
4. Dramatise inauguration of Kwame Nkrumah. [cite: 2147]
5. Produce chart of major political events 1951-1960. [cite: 2154]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.6.2.1 Analyse the main developments in the Republics between 1960 and 1972
indicator_description: Explain political developments under the first republic
examplars:
1. Identify main features of 1960 Constitution. [cite: 2154]
2. Examine changes resulting from the 1960 Constitution. [cite: 2159]
3. Debate effects of 1960 Republican Constitution changes. [cite: 2159]
4. Describe cabinet membership under 1960 Constitution. [cite: 2159]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.6.2.1 Analyse the main developments in the Republics between 1960 and 1972
indicator_description: Explain how the Second Republic came into being.
examplars:
1. Research how Second Republic was established. [cite: 2165]
2. Discuss outcome of 1969 general elections. [cite: 2165]
3. Dramatise handing over from NLC to Dr. K.A Busia. [cite: 2165]
4. Compare First and Second Republican Constitutions. [cite: 2165]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B8.6.2.1. Analyse the main developments in the Republics between 1960 and 1972
indicator_description: Explain political developments under the Second Republic
examplars:
1. Describe main features of 1969 Constitution. [cite: 2169]
2. Explain changes resulting from 1969 Constitution. [cite: 2169]
3. Discuss cabinet membership under 1969 Constitution. [cite: 2169]
